next_steps: T2: keep sq-fp8-materialize-smoke as the runtime artifact-boundary guard
T2: keep kup6_gate5_down5 only as a direct-logits regression subset; do not promote it as the SQ quality policy because the token-id model-loop prompt bundle fails strict top1 on len4 and case_a
T2: keep kup6_ogatedown5 as a near-miss failure guard
T2: use sq-fp8-kup6-gate5-down5-policy-v0.1.json through --policy-json as the current SQ policy representation for selected FP8 and fallback families
T2: use sq-fp8-format-candidate-matrix-v0.1.json as the current machine-readable SQ format candidate matrix
T2: treat /tmp/ullm-sq-fp8-kup6-gate5-down5-policy-v0.1-artifact as a reproducible boundary-check artifact, not a committed package or throughput artifact
T2: keep strict_top1 as the promotion rule until a text-level guard is implemented and accepted
T2: use top-k overlap, AQ4 top1 rank, logit gap, workload.final_topk_tokens, and workload.final_topk_logits as diagnostic-only fields

T2: use phase-t2-sq-fp8-token-id-model-loop-coverage-reduction-v1 as evidence that k/up row-block32 still fails case_a even at layer3-only coverage; single-tensor passes are not sufficient for policy promotion
T2: next quality iteration should add per-family/per-tensor scale-layout support, then test mixed scale policies such as k row-block16 + up row-block32 under the model-loop prompt-bundle gate
Planning: treat current prefill/cached-prefix attention speed as sufficient to resume SQ candidate evaluation; defer extra FlashAttention2-like work unless SQ comparison exposes a blocker
T1: use synthetic and package-backed component real-batch rows only for kernel/schema validation, not final SQ package throughput
T1/T2: use package-token-ids-model-loop-smoke as the current selected-layer bridge from token IDs to model-loop scheduler and final lm_head top1 guard
T1/T2: use phase-t1-token-id-model-loop-real-prefill-smoke-v1 as the current selected-layer request-batch prefill bridge; it is still not final full LM throughput
T2: use sq-fp8-token-ids-model-loop-smoke as the current SQ selected-layer bridge for AQ4/SQ top1 and schema comparison; it is still not final full LM throughput
T1: extend logical full-package rows to real full-package request-batch prefill/decode/end-to-end rows before using total throughput rows for SQ performance decisions
T5: use native FP8 or materialization-aware runtime paths for throughput comparison; do not use SQ overlay load timing as an SQ speed result
T5: move to AQ4/FP8 throughput comparison after the T1 full package total-throughput runner exists and a T2 model-loop-safe subset is encoded in the runtime path
T6/T7: run vLLM comparison only after uLLM R9700 batch=1/4/8 AQ4 and FP8 rows share the same schema

model_loop_coverage_reduction.finding: k/up row-block32 fails case_a even when reduced to layer3 only; up layer3 row-block32 and k layer3 row-block16 pass individually, but k/up layer3 row-block16 fails when combined

sq_candidate.initial_target: language model 2D projection, embedding, and lm_head weights

t0_t2_status.t1_component_real_batch_jsonl_parser_not: run-external-benchmark.py --parse ullm-component-prefill preserves batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, request/token parallelism, prefill total input tok/s, attention pair/s, and sampled correctness for component prefill rows.
t0_t2_status.t1_full_package_logical_grid_note: AQ4 full-package package-batch-throughput-bench rows are saved for batch=1/4/8. All rows are status=ok and verified_all=true, and preserve prefill/decode/end-to-end total throughput, KV cache bytes, VRAM, and correctness. These rows are logical batch only: batching.mode=logical, prefill_real_batch=false, decode_real_batch=false, runtime_reused_across_requests=false, and weights_reloaded_per_request=true. Real request-batch prefill/decode/end-to-end full-package rows are still required before SQ throughput comparison.
t0_t2_status.t1_jsonl_schema_preservation_note: run-external-benchmark.py preserves package-batch total-throughput metrics, prefix/chunk/context accounting, executor accounting, and memory.kv_cache_bytes_total in inference-benchmark-result-v0.1 JSONL rows.
t0_t2_status.t1_model_loop_hybrid_throughput_note: package-self-attn-mlp-block-model-loop-smoke emits timed prefill/decode/end-to-end token fields plus CSV workload fields; run-external-benchmark.py --parse ullm-model-loop-throughput converts it to inference-benchmark-result-v0.1. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke is a synthetic selected-layer stack row, not final full LM throughput. It preserves batching.mode=hybrid, prefill_real_batch=false, decode_real_batch=true, and decode_executor_request_parallelism=2.
t0_t2_status.t1_package_prefill_component_runner_note: tools/run-package-prefill-component-workload.py runs package-backed component smoke commands through run-external-benchmark.py --parse ullm-component-prefill; R9700 AQ4 k-proj smoke preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, executor=aq4_matvec_batch_f32, token_parallelism=2, and prefill_total_input_tokens_per_second=19063.596157. The flattened batch grid adds batch_size=4, prompt_tokens_per_request=[2,2,2,2], component_total_input_tokens=8, prefill_executor_token_parallelism=8, prefill_executor_request_parallelism=1, and prefill_total_input_tokens_per_second=65513.626834.
t0_t2_status.t1_token_id_model_loop_bridge_note: package-token-ids-model-loop-smoke feeds embedding token IDs into the selected-layer model-loop scheduler and emits final lm_head top1 tokens. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke used batch=2, prompt=2, generated=1 and preserved input_source=embedding_token_ids, batching.mode=hybrid, decode_real_batch=true, decode_executor_request_parallelism=2, final_top1_tokens=[155793,23175], and verified=true.
t0_t2_status.t1_token_id_model_loop_real_prefill_brid: package-token-ids-model-loop-smoke now groups prefill by layer/timestep across active requests and emits prefill_batch_request_counts_csv. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke used batch=2, prompt=2, generated=1 and preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, prefill_executor=stack_prefill_request_batch_step, prefill_batch_request_counts=[2,2,2,2], decode_real_batch=true, decode_executor_request_parallelism=2, final_top1_tokens=[155793,23175], and verified=true.
t0_t2_status.t2_artifact_metadata_path_note: build-sq-fp8-w8a16-artifact.py accepts --policy-json for sq-fp8-policy-v0.1; dry-run confirmed kup6_gate5_down5 resolves to 22 FP8 tensors, 753 passthrough tensors, and row_block32 scale. A real payload artifact was generated under /tmp with compact_resident_bytes_estimate=18579553248 and materialized_working_set_bytes_estimate=201326592.
t0_t2_status.t2_candidate_matrix_note: tools/build-sq-fp8-candidate-matrix.py builds a machine-readable candidate matrix from the current kup6_gate5_down5 policy and policy artifact result. It fixes W8A16 F32-scale, scale16, scale8, W8A8, and hybrid fallback candidate axes; strict top1 remains the promotion rule and overlay load timing remains excluded from speed results.
t0_t2_status.t2_runtime_load_smoke: sq-fp8-materialize-smoke cpu0/r9700 fixture path and r9700 policy artifact tensor path verified
t0_t2_status.t2_short_prompt_guard_note: One selected q_proj tensor and then the layer 3 projection set are loaded from SQ FP8 while the rest falls back to AQ4. The layer 3 projection-set guard matched AQ4 top1 in 3/3 short token-ID cases. Expanding to layers 3,7 changed top1 for attention-only, MLP-only, and attention+MLP overlays, while AQ4 top1 remained inside SQ top8. Family split shows q/v/down are risky for strict top1, while k/o/gate/up preserve top1 individually and as a combined subset across 3/3 short prompts. Expanding k/o/gate/up to layers 3,7,11,15 preserved top1 in 2/3 short prompts and failed case_a. Row-block scale recovers q at block32 and down at block64, but v remains strict-top1 risky for tested block sizes. A mixed candidate with v fallback and q/k/o/gate/up/down row-block32 FP8 passes layers 3,7,11,15 in 3/3 short prompts and layers 3,7,11,15,19 on len4. The same policy fails layers 3,7,11,15,19,23 and all self-attention probe layers. Layer 23 alone is fixed by q/v fallback, but 6-layer q/v fallback still fails, so cumulative drift remains. T2 promotion rule v0.1 is strict_top1; top-k/rank/gap are diagnostic-only. Six-layer family split shows k/up row-block32 passes strict top1 and k/up combined passes 3/3 short prompts, but o/gate/down fail individually at 6 layers. Per-layer combination search shows k/up all 6 plus any one or two of o/gate/down on layers 3,7,11,15,19 pass len4, while adding all three fails len4. The kup6_gate5_down5 prompt bundle passes len4, case_a, and case_b strict top1; case_a top8 overlap is only 2/8, so it is a six-layer regression subset rather than a full SQ policy. The selected FP8/fallback policy is saved as sq-fp8-kup6-gate5-down5-policy-v0.1 and was materialized into a real payload artifact for runtime boundary verification. Full-target SQ guard is still pending and needs broader coverage, stronger format decisions, or a formally accepted text-level guard. A stricter six-layer token-id model-loop prompt-bundle guard later showed that the same kup6_gate5_down5 artifact fails strict top1 on len4 and case_a when token-id embedding, selected runtime layers, request-batch prefill, decode ready batch, final norm, and lm_head are connected in one path; AQ4 top1 remains in SQ top8. Therefore the direct logits prompt-bundle pass is weaker evidence than the model-loop guard. Coverage reduction on the stricter model-loop gate then showed k/up row-block32 still fails case_a even when reduced to layer3 only; up_proj layer3 row-block32 and k_proj layer3 row-block16 pass individually, but k/up layer3 row-block16 fails when combined. Therefore the next T2 step is mixed per-family/per-tensor scale-layout support rather than widening k/up coverage.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_token_id_model_loop_bridge_note: sq-fp8-token-ids-model-loop-smoke connects the current kup6_gate5_down5 SQ FP8 artifact to the token-id model-loop request-batch prefill path. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke used batch=2, prompt=2, generated=1 and preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, decode_real_batch=true, sq_overlay=true, final_top1_tokens=[155793,23175], and verified=true. This is a selected-layer bridge, not final full LM throughput.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_token_id_model_loop_prompt_bundle_: sq-fp8-token-ids-model-loop-smoke was run on the current kup6_gate5_down5 SQ FP8 artifact with layers 3,7,11,15,19,23, batch=3, top_k=8, and len4/case_a/case_b prompt bundle. Both AQ4 and SQ rows preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, decode_real_batch=true, workload.final_topk_tokens, and workload.final_topk_logits. SQ matched AQ4 top1 only on case_b; len4 and case_a drifted while AQ4 top1 remained inside SQ top8 at ranks 3 and 2. Internal SQ tok/s was close to AQ4 on this selected-layer path, but wrapper elapsed includes artifact read/materialization. Under the current strict_top1 promotion rule, kup6_gate5_down5 remains a regression subset and is not promoted as the SQ quality policy.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_model_loop_coverage_reduction_note: R9700 token-id model-loop prompt bundle coverage reduction showed that k/up row-block32 still fails case_a from six layers down to layer3-only coverage. up_proj layer3 row-block32 and k_proj layer3 row-block16 pass strict top1 as single-tensor probes, but combined k/up layer3 row-block16 fails case_a. This blocks direct promotion and makes per-family/per-tensor scale-layout support the next T2 requirement.
